Function.Reverse transcriptase and endonuclease a: Reverse transcriptase and endonuclease activities provided by the LINE-1 ORF2p protein
Function.Reverse transcriptase inhibitor sensitiv: The characteristic of LINE-1 reverse transcriptase being sensitive to nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitors but resistant to non-nucleoside inhibitors
Function.Loss of hematopoietic stem cell function: The decline in self-renewal and differentiation capacity of hematopoietic stem cells caused by factors such as DNA damage
Function.Alteration of genetic network: Transposon insertion events that potentially disrupt interaction networks among genes
Function.Recombination mechanism: The process of gene rearrangement resulting from genomic DNA breaks and aberrant repair
Function.Heavy metal exposure affecting transposi: Exposure to heavy metals such as lead may directly alter the structural parameters of ORF1p, thereby influencing the overall retrotransposition frequency of LINE-1
Function.Lead ion binding: The ability of LINE-1 Open Reading Frame 1 protein (ORF1p) to bind multiple lead ions, utilizing its tri-cysteine layer as a metal-binding site
Function.Mismatch extension: The capacity of LINE-1 ORF2p reverse transcriptase to extend a DNA primer even when a base mismatch exists at its 3' end
Function.Mesenchymal-to-Epithelial Transition: A critical cell differentiation event during kidney development that can be disrupted by L1
Function.Indirect Replication of Non-autonomous R: LINE-1 facilitates the amplification of non-autonomous retrotransposons (e.g., SINEs) in the genome via its reverse transcription machinery
Function.Restriction of Retrotransposition: The suppression of retrotransposon mobility, such as LINE-1, by host factors like APOBEC3B
Function.Random Insertional Mutagenesis: Utilizing the retrotransposition activity of L1 elements as vectors to randomly insert into the genome and induce mutations
Function.Sequestration of Tumor Suppressors: The oncogenic mechanism whereby LINE-1-encoded proteins ORF1p and ORF2p bind to and sequester tumor suppressors
Function.Retrotransposition of Non-L1 Transcripts: Following displacement from its RNA template during TPRT, ORF1p may bind and disrupt residual L1 RNPs, rendering them susceptible to hijacking by non-L1 templates to mediate retrotransposition
Function.Production of Non-functional ORF1p Varia: Functionally impaired ORF1 proteins encoded by spliced retrotransposable elements integrated within the 5'UTR or ORF1 regions
Function.Non-reference L1 Polymorphic Insertions: L1 insertions present or absent among individuals that are not annotated in the current reference genome assembly
Function.Unconventional Translation Mechanisms: A protein translation mechanism that operates independently of the canonical AUG start codon
Function.Non-insertional Mechanisms: Mechanisms by which L1 elements exert effects without genomic insertion, such as modulating gene expression networks
Function.Non-coding RNA Regulation: Regulatory functions exerted by L1 RNA at the non-coding level, influencing cellular mitotic potential
Function.Target Genomic DNA Deletion: Loss of target genomic DNA fragments associated with endonuclease-independent LINE-1 insertion
Function.Cis/Trans-assisted Reverse Transcription: LINE-1 facilitates reverse transcription and genomic integration of other RNAs, such as mRNAs and small RNAs, in addition to self-replication
Function.Cis-acting: Provision of essential molecular machinery by LINE-1 for the retrotransposition of other mobile elements, such as Alu
Function.Cis-preference: The tendency of LINE-1 to preferentially retrotranspose its own concurrently translated transcripts
Function.Cis-mobilization: Preferential or exclusive mobilization of LINE-1-encoded RNA by its own protein machinery
Function.Provision of Cis-regulatory Elements: Host co-option of LINE-1 sequences to supply regulatory elements for gene expression
Function.Cis-transposition: LINE-1 reverse transcribes its own RNA copies and inserts them into new genomic locations
Function.Cis-retrotransposition: LINE-1 mediates the retrotransposition of its own sequences
Function.Driving Alu and SVA Transposition: The LINE-1 retrotransposition machinery drives the cis-mobilization of non-autonomous Alu and SVA transposons
Function.Driver Mutation: Mutations that promote tumorigenesis, progression, and metastasis
Function.High Processivity: LINE-1 reverse transcriptase exhibits higher processivity in vitro compared to Moloney murine leukemia virus reverse transcriptase
Function.Efficient Delivery: The effective introduction of genetic material into target cells
Function.High-throughput Dual-luciferase Assay: A high-sensitivity, wide-dynamic-range functional assay using firefly luciferase as a retrotransposition reporter and Renilla luciferase for normalization
